headline: Google Breakthrough Spurs Chip Selloff Despite Analyst Doubt
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Shares of computer memory and storage products slumped on concerns over demand after Google researchers touted a new compression technique. But it may be a hiccup rather than an existential threat.SK Hynix Inc., a key maker of memory chips for artificial intelligence applications, fell as much as 6.4% on the Korea Exchange. Flash memory manufacturer Kioxia Holdings Corp. dropped 6.4% in Tokyo. That followed losses by Micron Technology Inc. and Sandisk Corp. Wednesday in New York.T

headline: SK Hynix Takes AI Ambitions to Wall Street with Potential $14 Billion U.S. Listing
summary: South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix said it has filed confidentially for a U.S. listing that could raise up to $14 billion. The planned listing comes as SK Hynix ramps up investment to meet strong demand for AI data center chips. headline: Milestone grows net revenue to USD 340 million in 2025 and invests in the intelligent video era
summary: Milestone Systems, a global leader in data driven video technology, continued to grow in 2025, increasing net revenue by 10% to USD 340 million (DKK 2.2 billion). Nearly a third was reinvested in innovation fueling new advances in video analytics, responsible AI and cloud technology, The year was also marked by collaboration with NVIDIA and the launch of Project Hafnia.

headline: Chip Selloff Deepens After Google Touts Memory Breakthrough
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Memory chip stocks extended their losses on Thursday after Alphabet Inc.’s Google publicized research that could allow more efficient use of the storage needed for artificial intelligence development.SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co., South Korean leaders in the market, fell more than 6% and about 5% in Seoul. headline: Google, Meta ruling could be a Tobacco Moment for social media cases
summary: The "Magnificent Seven" (MAGS) stocks are all down from their 52-week highs. Additionally, Meta (META) and Google (GOOG, GOOGL) were just found liable for damages in a landmark social media addiction lawsuit.Slatestone Wealth chief market strategist Kenny Polcari explains how investors should approach buying into the Big Tech names, and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferre emphasizes how this could be the "tobacco moment" for social media–focused Mag Seven names.

content.title: Google, Meta ruling could be a Tobacco Moment for social media cases
content.description:
The "Magnificent Seven" (MAGS) stocks are all down from their 52-week highs. Additionally, Meta (META) and Google (GOOG, GOOGL) were just found liable for damages in a landmark social media addiction lawsuit.

Slatestone Wealth chief market strategist Kenny Polcari explains how investors should approach buying into the Big Tech names, and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferre emphasizes how this could be the "tobacco moment" for social media–focused Mag Seven names.
content.summary: The "Magnificent Seven" (MAGS) stocks are all down from their 52-week highs. Additionally, Meta (META) and Google (GOOG, GOOGL) were just found liable for damages in a landmark social media addiction lawsuit.Slatestone Wealth chief market strategist Kenny Polcari explains how investors should approach buying into the Big Tech names, and Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferre emphasizes how this could be the "tobacco moment" for social media–focused Mag Seven names.
